Nomad [![License: BUSL-1.1](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-BUSL--1.1-yellow.svg)](LICENSE) [![Discuss](https://img.shields.io/badge/discuss-nomad-00BC7F?style=flat)](https://discuss.hashicorp.com/c/nomad) ===

Nomad is a simple and flexible workload orchestrator to deploy and manage containers ([docker](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/deploy/task-driver/docker), [podman](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/plugins/drivers/podman)), non-containerized applications ([executable](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/deploy/task-driver/exec), [Java](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/deploy/task-driver/java)), and virtual machines ([qemu](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/deploy/task-driver/qemu)) across on-prem and clouds at scale. Nomad is supported on Linux, Windows, and macOS. A commercial version of Nomad, [Nomad Enterprise](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/enterprise), is also available. * [Documentation - concepts, user guides, reference](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs) * [CLI docs](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/commands) * [API docs](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/api-docs) * [Nomad plugins docs](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/plugins) * [Tutorials](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/tutorials) * Forum: [Discuss](https://discuss.hashicorp.com/c/nomad) Nomad provides several key features: * **Deploy Containers and Legacy Applications**: Nomad’s flexibility as an orchestrator enables an organization to run containers, legacy, and batch applications together on the same infrastructure. Nomad brings core orchestration benefits to legacy applications without needing to containerize via pluggable task drivers. * **Simple & Reliable**: Nomad runs as a single binary and is entirely self contained - combining resource management and scheduling into a single system. Nomad does not require any external services for storage or coordination. Nomad automatically handles application, node, and driver failures. Nomad is distributed and resilient, using leader election and state replication to provide high availability in the event of failures. * **Device Plugins & GPU Support**: Nomad offers built-in support for GPU workloads such as machine learning (ML) and artificial intelligence (AI). Nomad uses device plugins to automatically detect and utilize resources from hardware devices such as GPU, FPGAs, and TPUs.

content.text:
I completed all seven steps. Here is the audit.

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** HashiCorp Nomad, a cluster scheduler/orchestrator (Go), BUSL-1.1. Single binary that runs as agent → server and/or client.

**Layout:**
- `nomad/` — the **server** package (the object being reworked). RPC endpoints, Raft FSM, leadership, scheduling workers.
- `nomad/<subpkg>/` — subsystems deliberately kept out of the server package to avoid import cycles: `deploymentwatcher/`, `drainer/`, `volumewatcher/`, `auth/`, `state/`, `stream/`, `lock/`, `peers/`, `reporting/`, `structs/`, `mock/`.
- `scheduler/` — pluggable schedulers (`generic_sched.go`, `scheduler_system.go`, `scheduler_sysbatch.go`) + `scheduler/structs/` (the `State`/`Planner`/`Scheduler` interfaces), `scheduler/feasible/`, `scheduler/reconciler/`.
- `client/` — Nomad client agent. `command/agent/` — the agent that *owns* a `*nomad.Server` and the HTTP API. `api/` — Go SDK. `helper/`, `plugins/`, `drivers/`, `ui/`, `e2e/`.

**The object:** `type Server struct` at `nomad/server.go:116`, built by `NewServer` at `nomad/server.go:336`, torn down by `Shutdown` at `nomad/server.go:744`. It is owned by the agent at `command/agent/agent.go:113`.

## Step 2 — The lifecycle contract

**Construction** — `nomad/server.go:336` `NewServer(config, consulCatalog, consulConfigFunc)`. Ordered wiring inside:
- `nomad/server.go:378-380` — `workersEventCh`, `lockTTLTimer`, `lockDelayTimer` allocated
- `nomad/server.go:401` `evalBroker`; `:404` `blockedEvals`
- `nomad/server.go:407` `s.rpcHandler = newRpcHandler(s)`
- `nomad/server.go:410-415` `newPlanner(s)`, `newNodeHeartbeater(s)`
- `nomad/server.go:420` `NewPeriodicDispatch(s.logger, s)`
- `nomad/server.go:423-424` `NewStatsFetcher(...)` + `SetLocalServer(s)`
- `nomad/server.go:437` `NewEncrypter(s, keystorePath)`
- `nomad/server.go:469` `setupRPC` → `:1296 setupRpcServer`, `:1268 setupStreamingEndpoints`
- `nomad/server.go:475-483` `auth.NewAuthenticator{StateFn: s.State, GetLeaderACLFn: s.getLeaderAcl, Encrypter: s.encrypter}`
- `nomad/server.go:486` `setupRaft` → `:1345`, builds `FSMConfig` (`nomad/fsm.go:168`) with `Periodic: s.periodicDispatcher` (`server.go:1359`), EvalBroker, Blocked, Encrypter
- `nomad/server.go:493` `setupSerf`; `:501` `setupWorkers` → `:1944 setupWorkersLocked` → `:1971 NewWorker(ctx, s, poolArgs)`
- `nomad/server.go:508` `setupConsulSyncer` → `:962 setupBootstrapHandler` (retains a `bootstrapFn` closure over `s` in a goroutine, `server.go:1130-1137`)
- `nomad/server.go:514` `setupDeploymentWatcher` → `:1156`
- `nomad/server.go:520` `setupVolumeWatcher` → `:1178`
- `nomad/server.go:531` `setupNodeDrainer` → `:1187`
- `nomad/server.go:534` `setupEnterprise` → `nomad/server_setup_ce.go:22` (creates `AutopilotDelegate{s}`, handed to the raft-autopilot library which retains it)
- `nomad/server.go:571` `NewKeyringReplicator(s, encrypter)`
- `nomad/server.go:536-540` `go s.monitorLeadership()`, `go s.serfEventHandler()`, `startRPCListener()`

**Shutdown** — `nomad/server.go:744 Shutdown()`: sets `shutdown`, `shutdownCancel()`, stops workers (`:757-761`), serf, raft+transport+store, RPC listener, conn pool, FSM close, `consulConfigEntries.Stop()`, `oidcProviderCache.Shutdown()`. **It does not nil out or notify any of the retained handles below.** `nomad/server.go:815 Leave()`, `nomad/server.go:910 Reload()`.

**State outliving a request:** raft/FSM/StateStore (`server.go:143,147`, `fsm.go:138`), evalBroker/blockedEvals (`server.go:205,209`), planQueue+badNodeTracker (`plan_apply.go:28,35`), periodicDispatcher (`server.go:254`), encrypter + keyring (`server.go:250,246`), deploymentWatcher/nodeDrainer/volumeWatcher (`server.go:230,233,236`), workers (`server.go:271`), nodeConns (`server.go:181`), lock timers (`server.go:290-291`), leaderAcl (`server.go:295`), oidc caches (`server.go:279,284`).

**Layers between an incoming RPC and that state:**
1. `nomad/rpc.go:196 (r *rpcHandler).listen` — accept loop
2. `nomad/rpc.go:280 handleConn` / `:418 handleMultiplex` / `:524 handleMultiplexV2` — per-conn demux; calls `setupRpcServer(server, rpcCtx)` so endpoint structs are rebuilt **per connection**
3. `nomad/server.go:1296 setupRpcServer` — endpoint registration (each endpoint captures `s`)
4. endpoint method, e.g. `nomad/job_endpoint.go:92 Job.Register` — `j.srv.Authenticate` / `j.srv.forward`
5. `nomad/rpc.go:586 (r *rpcHandler).forward` — leader/region forwarding
6. `nomad/auth/auth.go` `Authenticator` — ACL/identity resolution via `getState`/`encrypter`
7. read path: `nomad/rpc.go:854 blockingRPC` → `nomad/server.go:2115 (s *Server).State()` → `s.fsm.State()`
8. write path: `nomad/rpc.go:811 (s *Server).raftApply` → raft → `nomad/fsm.go Apply`

## Step 3 — How server access reaches a subsystem (worked trace: the node drainer)

1. `nomad/server.go:531` — `NewServer` calls `s.setupNodeDrainer()`
2. `nomad/server.go:1187-1189` — `setupNodeDrainer` creates `shim := drainerShim{s}` (the narrowing step)
3. `nomad/drainer_shims.go:14-16` — `type drainerShim struct { s *Server }`; methods `NodesDrainComplete` (`:19`) and `AllocUpdateDesiredTransition` (`:38`) call `d.s.raftApply` (`nomad/rpc.go:811`)
4. `nomad/server.go:1190-1198` — shim is placed in `drainer.NodeDrainerConfig{Raft: shim}` (`nomad/drainer/drainer.go:114-116`), typed as `drainer.RaftApplier` (`nomad/drainer/drainer.go:48`)
5. `nomad/server.go:1199` — `s.nodeDrainer = drainer.NewNodeDrainer(c)`
6. **Retained** at `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:159` — `raft RaftApplier`. The `drainer` package never imports `nomad` and never names `*Server`.
7. Activated later at `nomad/leader.go:416` — `s.nodeDrainer.SetEnabled(true, s.State())`, which also hands over a `*state.StateStore` retained at `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:153`.

## Step 6 — Blast radius, ranked by risk

**Tier 1 — no compile-time tie, silent breakage:**
1. `nomad/auth/auth.go:52-53` (D3/D4). Bound method values pin the *old* `*Server` forever; a replaced server keeps authenticating against the dead one's state store and leader ACL. Nothing in `Shutdown` (`server.go:744`) clears `s.auth`. **Highest risk: a stale ACL/state view is a security bug, not just a leak.** Also interacts with `Reload` (`server.go:910`) which mutates `verifyTLS`.
2. `nomad/deploymentwatcher/multiregion_ce.go:13-14` (D7/D8/D11/D12). `DeploymentRPC`/`JobRPC` are **empty interfaces in CE** — any value type-checks. A lifecycle rework can pass the wrong/dead object and CE compiles clean; only ENT would catch it.
3. `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:76` + `deployment_watcher_shims.go:15` (D6/D9/D10). The shim stores a raw `func` bound to the old server's raft handle; after `Shutdown` closes raft (`server.go:768-776`), applies fail late and opaquely. Fanned out into per-deployment watchers and the batcher, so one stale shim multiplies.
4. `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:159` (D13) and `nomad/volumewatcher/volumes_watcher.go:26` + `volume_watcher.go:30` (D14/D15). Same shape; the volume watcher's handle is *copied into every child watcher*, so re-wiring the parent alone is insufficient.
5. `nomad/periodic.go:27` (D1) — **double retention**: the dispatcher is held by the server *and* by the FSM (`fsm.go:135`), and the FSM survives raft `Restore()`. A rewired server with the old FSM leaves a dispatcher dispatching into the dead server.

**Tier 2 — narrowed but locally visible:**
6. `nomad/variables_endpoint.go:52` (D2) — a per-connection endpoint holding `srv` twice (as `srv` and as `timers`); the lock timers themselves live on the server (`server.go:290-291`) and are leader-only state.
7. `scheduler/generic_sched.go:57`, `scheduler_system.go:37`, `scheduler_sysbatch.go:33`, `core_sched.go:35` (D16-D19) — reach the server through `*Worker`. Workers are stopped on `Shutdown` (`server.go:757-761`) and rebuilt on config reload (`server.go:1985 setupNewWorkersLocked`, `:2007 stopOldWorkers`), so an in-flight scheduler can outlive its worker's server.
8. `scheduler/*.eventsCh` + `feasible/context.go:97` (E1-E4) and `job_endpoint.go:1897` (E5) — hold `s.workersEventCh`. If the rework recreates the channel, in-flight schedulers write to a channel nobody drains (`server.go:2035`) — a goroutine leak, not a compile error.
9. `command/agent/http.go:102` (D20) — HTTP layer resolves the server lazily through `RPCer.Server()`; it will see whatever `Agent.server` (`agent.go:113`) points at, so it is safe *only if* the agent is the single source of truth after the rework.

**Tier 3 — direct `*Server`, compiler-assisted but numerous:**
10. Area B (32 endpoints). Rebuilt per connection at `server.go:1296` via `rpc.go:280 handleConn`, **but** the ones registered at startup into `s.rpcServer` (`server.go:1205`) and the streaming set (`server.go:1268-1290`) are process-lifetime. Three of them (`CSIVolume`, `Deployment`, `Job`) are additionally handed to watchers at `server.go:1168,1169,1180` and live as long as those watchers.
11. Area C (9 hooks). Rebuilt with each `Job` endpoint, so they inherit the endpoint's lifetime — verify `job_endpoint.go:66-88` after any change to endpoint construction.
12. Area A. `AutopilotDelegate` (A4) is the sneakiest: handed to the third-party `autopilot.New` (`server_setup_ce.go:26`) which retains it and **is never shut down in `Shutdown()`**. `StatsFetcher.localServer` (A5) is set through a *setter* (`stats_fetcher.go:48`), not a constructor — easy to miss. `KeyringReplicator` (A7) has its own `stopFn` (`encrypter.go:1116`) independent of server shutdown.
13. `A11` bootstrap closure (`server.go:996`) — only exits on `s.shutdownCh`; if the rework changes how `shutdownCh` is derived (`server.go:339-341`), this goroutine leaks with a live server reference.
14. ENT-only: `NewEnterpriseEndpoints(s, ctx)` (`server.go:1342`, stub at `endpoints_ce.go:16`) and `EnterpriseState` (`server.go:320`, `server_setup_ce.go:12`) — must be re-audited in the enterprise branch, which this repo cannot verify.

**Must be re-verified after the change:** `establishLeadership` (`leader.go:378`, esp. `:413,:416,:419,:430`) and `revokeLeadership` (`leader.go:1422`, esp. `:1445,:1448,:1451,:1454`) — the only places watchers get/lose their state store; `Shutdown` (`server.go:744`) must be extended to detach or invalidate D1-D15; `Reload` (`server.go:910`) and `setupNewWorkersLocked` (`server.go:1985`) for the worker/scheduler chain.
